description: Diplacus aurantiacus, the sticky monkey-flower or orange bush monkey-flower, is a flowering plant that grows in a subshrub form, native to western North America from southwestern Oregon south through Northern and Central California. It is a member of the lopseed family, Phrymaceae, and was formerly known as Mimulus aurantiacus.
origin: NORTHERN AMERICA, NORTHWESTERN USA, CAL OO, SOUTHWESTERN USA, CAL
synonyms: ["Mimulus viscosus", "Diplacus glutinosus var. aurantiacus", "Diplacus glutinosus var. latifolius", "Diplacus glutinosus", "Diplacus aurantius", "Diplacus longiflorus var. grandiflorus", "Diplacus splendidus", "Mimulus leptanthus", "Diplacus godronii", "Diplacus leptanthus"]

description: Diplacus bigelovii is a species of monkeyflower known by the common name Bigelow's monkeyflower. It is native to the southwestern United States, where it grows in desert and slope habitats. It was formerly known as Mimulus bigelovii.
origin: MEXICO, NEV, MXN BC, CAL OO, MXN BS

description: Diplacus brevipes is a species of monkeyflower known by the common name widethroat yellow monkeyflower. It was formerly known as Mimulus brevipes.
origin: MEXICO, MAS OO, MXN BC, MAS, CAL OO

description: Diplacus longiflorus, commonly known as southern bush monkeyflower, is a species of monkeyflower native to southwestern California and Baja California Norte (Mexico).
origin: MEXICO, MXN BC, NORTHERN AMERICA, MXN BS, CAL OO
synonyms: ["Diplacus glutinosus var. pubescens", "Mimulus glutinosus var. brachypus", "Diplacus arachnoideus", "Diplacus speciosus", "Mimulus aurantiacus var. pubescens", "Diplacus aurantiacus var. longiflorus", "Mimulus longiflorus"]

description: Diplacus mephiticus is a species of monkeyflower known by the common names skunky monkeyflower and foul odor monkeyflower. It was formerly known as Mimulus mephiticus.
origin: NEV, NORTHERN AMERICA, CAL OO, SOUTHWESTERN USA, CAL
synonyms: ["Mimulus densus", "Mimulus wolfii", "Mimulus coccineus var. wolfii", "Mimulus nanus var. mephiticus", "Mimulus mephiticus", "Eunanus angustifolius", "Mimulus stamineus", "Mimulus coccineus", "Mimulus angustifolius", "Eunanus mephiticus"]

description: Diplacus puniceus, commonly known as the red bush monkeyflower, San Diego monkey flower, or mission diplacus, is a species of perennial shrub native to coastal southern California and northern Baja California. It is characterized by a relatively small and broad corolla for Diplacus, a lack of glandular hairs, and dark orange or red flowers, adapted to hummingbird pollinators.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, MXN SI, SOUTHWESTERN USA, MEXICO
synonyms: ["Mimulus puniceus", "Mimulus aurantiacus var. puniceus", "Diplacus aurantiacus var. puniceus", "Diplacus glutinosus var. puniceus", "Mimulus glutinosus var. puniceus"]

description: Diplacus torreyi is a species of monkeyflower known by the common name Torrey's monkeyflower.
origin: SOUTHWESTERN USA, NORTHERN AMERICA, CAL OO, CAL

description: Erythranthe alsinoides is a species of monkeyflower known by the common names wingstem monkeyflower and chickweed monkeyflower. It was formerly known as Mimulus alsinoides.
origin: WAS OO, NORTHWESTERN USA, ORE OO, CAL, WESTERN CANADA
synonyms: ["Mimulus alsinoides", "Mimulus alsinoides var. paniculatus", "Mimulus alsinoides var. minimus"]

description: Erythranthe breweri is a species of monkeyflower known by the common name Brewer's monkeyflower. It is native to western North America from British Columbia to California to Colorado, where it grows in moist spots in several habitat types. This is a hairy annual herb producing a thin, erect stem up to 21 centimeters tall. The herbage is reddish green in color. The paired opposite leaves are linear in shape and up to 3.5 centimeters long.

Erythranthe floribunda is a species of monkeyflower known by the common name many-flowered monkeyflower. It is native to western North America from western Canada to California and northern Mexico, to the Rocky Mountains. It grows in many types of habitat, especially moist areas. It was formerly known as Mimulus floribundus.
origin: MEXICO, NEV, MXN BC, ABT, MXN BS
synonyms: ["Mimulus floribundus var. membranaceus", "Mimulus subulatus", "Mimulus floribundus var. minor", "Mimulus multiflorus", "Mimulus floribundus var. subulatus", "Mimulus trisulcatus", "Mimulus deltoideus", "Mimulus serotinus", "Mimulus membranaceus", "Capraria pusilla"]

origin: NIC, AGS RN, AGW CA, MXC TL, CLN AT

description: Erythranthe guttata, with the common names seep monkeyflower and common yellow monkeyflower, is a yellow bee-pollinated annual or perennial flowering plant. It was formerly known as Mimulus guttatus.
origin: ITA VC, RUW OO, EASTERN CANADA, NORTHEASTERN USA, NOR OO
synonyms: ["Mimulus guttatus var. gracilior", "Mimulus whipplei", "Mimulus guttatus var. depauperatus", "Mimulus glabratus var. adscendens", "Mimulus guttatus", "Mimulus guttatus subsp. typicus", "Mimulus langsdorffii var. guttatus", "Mimulus hirsutus", "Mimulus lyratus", "Mimulus langsdorffii"]

description: Erythranthe lewisii is a perennial plant in the family Phrymaceae. It is named in honor of explorer Meriwether Lewis. Together with other species in Erythranthe, it serves as a model system for studying pollinator-based reproductive isolation. It was formerly known as Mimulus lewisii.
origin: UTA, ABT OO, ASK, CAL, IDA OO
synonyms: ["Mimulus lewisii f. tetonensis", "Mimulus lewisii var. tetonensis", "Mimulus roseus", "Mimulus lewisii f. albus", "Mimulus lewisii f. leuceruthrus", "Mimulus lewisii", "Mimulus roseus var. glabrior", "Mimulus lewisii var. exsertus", "Mimulus lewisii var. albus"]

description: Erythranthe lutea is a species of monkeyflower also known as yellow monkeyflower, monkey musk, blotched monkey flowers, and blood-drop-emlets. It was formerly known as Mimulus luteus. It is a perennial native to temperate South America, including Chile and western and southern Argentina.
origin: AGW SL, AGS, CLN AN, AGW JU, AGS SC

description: Erythranthe moschata is a species of monkeyflower known by the common names muskflower, musk monkeyflower, and formerly as the common musk, eyebright and musk plant. It was formerly known as Mimulus moschatus.
origin: ITA VC, OFS, EASTERN CANADA, NORTHEASTERN USA, RUC OO
synonyms: ["Mimulus acutidens", "Mimulus guttatus var. moschatus", "Mimulus leibergii", "Mimulus moniliformis", "Mimulus crinitus", "Erythranthe inodora", "Mimulus macranthus", "Mimulus moschatus var. moniliformis", "Mimulus moschatus var. longiflorus", "Mimulus moschatus var. pallidiflorus"]

description: Erythranthe nasuta, also known as shy monkeyflower, is a species of monkeyflower. It was formerly known as Mimulus nasutus. It is an annual native to western North America, ranging from British Columbia to northwestern Mexico.

origin: MYA, CHN SA, CHS HN, INDIAN SUBCONTINENT, ASS ME
synonyms: ["Mimulus assamicus", "Mimulus nepalensis", "Torenia arisanensis", "Mimulus tenellus subsp. nepalensis", "Mimulus tenellus f. taiwaniana", "Mimulus tenellus var. nepalensis", "Mimulus formosanus"]

description: Erythranthe primuloides is a Western United States perennial plant in the lopseed family (Phrymaceae), known by the common name primrose monkeyflower. It was formerly known as Mimulus primuloides.
origin: MEXICO, NEV, MXN BC, NORTHWESTERN USA, MXN BS
synonyms: ["Mimulus primuloides var. minimus", "Mimulus primuloides var. pilosellus", "Mimulus nevadensis", "Mimulus pilosellus", "Mimulus primuloides"]

description: Erythranthe tilingii is a species of monkeyflower known by the common name Tiling's monkeyflower. It was formerly known as Mimulus tilingii.

Mimetanthe is a genus of flowering plants in the family Phrymaceae. It has only one species, Mimetanthe pilosa, synonym Mimulus pilosus, known by the common names false monkeyflower and downy mimetanthe. It is native to the western United States and Baja California, where it grows in moist and disturbed habitat types. This plant is different enough from other monkeyflowers that it is treated in its own monotypic genus, Mimetanthe, or it may be retained in Mimulus.

Mimulus alatus, the sharpwing monkeyflower, is an herbaceous eudicot perennial that has no floral scent. It is native to North America and its blooming season is from June to September. The flowering plant has green foliage and blue to violet flowers. It has a short life span compared to most other plants and a rapid growth rate. Like other monkey-flowers of the genus Mimulus, M. alatus grows best in wet to moist conditions and has a bilabiate corolla, meaning it is two-lipped.
Mimulus gracilis the slender monkey flower is a species of monkey flower found in Africa and Australia.
Mimulus ringens is a species of monkeyflower known by the common names Allegheny monkeyflower and square-stemmed monkeyflower.
Phryma leptostachya, or lopseed, is a perennial herb of the genus Phryma. When distinguished from Phryma oblongifolia and Phryma nana, it is native to eastern North America.
